lines: -Fixes: #9795 Delete orphaned compression_settings before migrating catalog table
-Fixes: #9799 Fix job_errors view leaking failed jobs to non-owners
-Thanks: @homanp for reporting an information leak with the job_errors view
-Fixes: #9824 Adding migration scripts for composite bloom filters
-Fixes: #9828 Skip columnar index scan when grouping by an expression
-Fixes: #9830 Skip ColumnarIndexScan for GROUPING SETS / ROLLUP / CUBE

**Please note: When updating your database, you should connect using `psql` with the `-X` flag to prevent any `.psqlrc` commands from accidentally triggering the load of a previous TimescaleDB version.**
+This release contains performance improvements and bug fixes since the 2.27.0 release. We recommend that you upgrade at the next available opportunity.
+* [#9795](https://github.com/timescale/timescaledb/pull/9795) Delete orphaned `compression_settings` before migrating catalog table
+* [#9799](https://github.com/timescale/timescaledb/pull/9799) Fix `job_errors` view leaking failed jobs to non-owners
+* [#9824](https://github.com/timescale/timescaledb/pull/9824) Adding migration scripts for composite bloom filters
+* [#9828](https://github.com/timescale/timescaledb/pull/9828) Skip columnar index scan when grouping by an expression
+* [#9830](https://github.com/timescale/timescaledb/pull/9830) Skip `ColumnarIndexScan` for GROUPING SETS / ROLLUP / CUBE
+* @homanp for reporting an information leak with the job_errors view
This release contains performance improvements and bug fixes since the 2.26.4 release. We recommend that you upgrade at the next available opportunity.
# The downgrade file to generate a downgrade script for the current version, as
+-- The naming scheme for the composite bloom filter metadata columns has changed in 2.27.
+-- See the commit message for more details and the bug report here:
+        -- Make sure the old meta name actually exists for the compressed chunk
+        -- relation, so the renaming that follows only impact real columns not
+            -- Calculate the old and new metadata column names of the composite bloom filters.
+            -- Note that the new scheme always use a hash string to distinguish between the
+            -- composite columns, but the old one only used the hash if the concatenated column
+                -- Calculate the first 4 characters of the md5 hashes of both the
+                -- zero and underscore concatenated column names of the composite
+                    -- Select the compression settings objects that are actually a
+                    -- a 'bloom' filter, out of the already selected 'column' arrays
+                    -- and return the compressed chunk relation along with the column
+                    -- names concatenated with underscores as well as zeroes.
+                        -- Select the settings where the column field is an array
+                        -- which is a must for the composite bloom filters
+                            -- Capture the compression settings for the compressed
+                            -- tables, and separate the individual settings along
+        WHERE att.attrelid = e.compress_relid::regclass AND att.attname = e.old_meta_name
+-- The naming scheme for the composite bloom filter metadata columns has changed in 2.27.
+-- See the commit message for more details and the bug report here:
+        -- Make sure the old meta name actually exists for the compressed chunk
+        -- relation, so the renaming that follows only impact real columns not
+            count(*) OVER (PARTITION BY att.attrelid, e.old_meta_name) AS n
+            -- Calculate the old and new metadata column names of the composite bloom filters.
+            -- Note that the new scheme always use a hash string to distinguish between the
+            -- composite columns, but the old one only used the hash if the concatenated column
+                -- Calculate the first 4 characters of the md5 hashes of both the
+                -- zero and underscore concatenated column names of the composite
+                    -- Select the compression settings objects that are actually a
+                    -- a 'bloom' filter, out of the already selected 'column' arrays
+                    -- and return the compressed chunk relation along with the column
+                    -- names concatenated with underscores as well as zeroes.
+                        -- Select the settings where the column field is an array
+                        -- which is a must for the composite bloom filters
+                            -- Capture the compression settings for the compressed
+                            -- tables, and separate the individual settings along
+        WHERE att.attrelid = e.compress_relid::regclass AND att.attname = e.new_meta_name

+            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Downgrade is not possible because ambiguous composite bloom filters found. The ambiguous composite bloom filter column % for relation % need to be removed with the ALTER command.',
